records.name: npm Workspaces
records.usage: Workspace package manager for the Phase 0 monorepo scaffold.
records.replacementPath: pnpm or another workspace manager if release tooling requires it.
records.notes: Selected to avoid adding an additional package-manager dependency during Phase 0.

records.name: Electron
records.usage: Desktop shell and main/preload/renderer runtime.
records.replacementPath: Keep renderer isolation strong for future shell migration.
records.notes: Pinned for scaffold reproducibility.

records.name: React
records.usage: Renderer UI framework.
records.replacementPath: Keep UI components app-owned.

records.name: React DOM
records.usage: Renderer DOM mounting for React.
records.replacementPath: Replace with renderer framework adapter if React is replaced.

records.name: @types/react
records.usage: Type declarations for React renderer code.
records.replacementPath: Remove only if React ships sufficient built-in types for Pige usage.

records.name: @types/react-dom
records.usage: Type declarations for React DOM renderer code.
records.replacementPath: Remove only if React DOM ships sufficient built-in types for Pige usage.

records.name: Vite
records.usage: Renderer and app development/build pipeline.
records.replacementPath: Keep config conventional enough to replace.

records.name: @vitejs/plugin-react
records.usage: React transform plugin for Vite renderer builds.
records.replacementPath: Remove if renderer framework or build pipeline changes.
records.notes: Pinned with Vite 7 because electron-vite 5 peer range does not yet allow Vite 8.

records.name: TypeScript
records.usage: Primary type-checking and declarations for app/packages.
records.replacementPath: Keep compatibility path if ecosystem tooling requires TypeScript 6.

records.name: electron-vite
records.usage: Electron/Vite integration for scaffolded app builds.
records.replacementPath: Replace with conventional Vite/Electron config if packaging requires it.

records.name: electron-builder
records.usage: Build the macOS and Windows application bundles and release artifacts, with signing, notarization, and update-metadata hooks retained for release hardening.
records.replacementPath: Keep packaging configuration app-owned so a reviewed Electron packaging/signing pipeline can replace electron-builder without changing product-domain services.
records.notes: Direct build-only pin. npm lock integrity: sha512-a1KM5heqS3gQCZzizXEI8RjJy3QVogULPdeSknt76uLDpBIW/HDGsMg/XgP0riP6PI9COsRvFITKKGDqA8fJxA==. Excluded from the packaged runtime SBOM.

records.name: @electron/asar
records.usage: List and extract the built ASAR for bounded packageability verification of runtime entries and native unpacked resources.
records.replacementPath: Remove the direct verifier dependency if the selected packaging pipeline exposes an equally bounded, tested ASAR inspection interface.
records.notes: Direct build-only pin. npm lock integrity: sha512-npW1NW5yy8EB9XY/vEw9sUdgmq0sJEhmSBb6bqyFOAw1CSkrhvAvO6QWlW8CdIMo8VN1lkdF345l/MeW0LrY0Q==. Excluded from runtime authority and the packaged runtime SBOM.

records.name: Vitest
records.usage: Unit test runner for Phase 0 package tests.
records.replacementPath: Replace through test harness adapter if needed.

records.name: Zod
records.usage: Shared schema validation for manifests and future DTO/frontmatter boundaries.
records.replacementPath: Replace with another schema library behind packages/schemas.

records.name: SQLite
records.usage: Local rebuildable database engine for page metadata, FTS search, graph indexes, jobs, chunks, and memory index state.
records.replacementPath: Keep database state rebuildable so Pige can change SQLite driver/runtime without rewriting durable knowledge.
records.notes: Actual SQLite version should be reported by diagnostics once platform smoke coverage is added.

records.name: Node node:sqlite
records.usage: Initial v0.1 SQLite driver for Local Database Service, using the SQLite build bundled with the pinned Electron/Node runtime.
records.replacementPath: Switch to better-sqlite3 behind LocalDatabaseDriver if node:sqlite stability, packaging, or performance is insufficient.
records.notes: Node 24 marks this API experimental; release gates must include platform DB smoke tests.

records.name: proper-lockfile
records.usage: Acquire and refresh the main-process per-vault writer lease before mutable vault services and startup reconciliation begin.
records.replacementPath: Replace behind VaultWriterLease without changing durable Job or vault schemas.
records.notes: Integrity sha512-TjNPblN4BwAWMXU8s9AEz4JmQxnD1NNL7bNOY/AKUzyamc379FWASUhc/K1pL2noVb+XmZKLL68cjzLsiOAMaA==. Pige adds canonical vault/root and lock-directory inode fencing, opaque owner tokens, and exact-record Job claims; no network or durable payload is involved.

records.name: graceful-fs
records.usage: Transitive filesystem compatibility layer used by the pinned proper-lockfile runtime.
records.replacementPath: Moves only with removal or upgrade of proper-lockfile.
records.notes: Integrity sha512-RbJ5/jmFcNNCcDV5o9eTnBLJ/HszWV0P73bc+Ff4nS/rJj+YaS6IGyiOL0VoBYX+l1Wrl3k63h/KrH+nhJ0XvQ==. Runtime transitive only.

records.name: retry
records.usage: Transitive bounded retry primitive used by the pinned proper-lockfile runtime.
records.replacementPath: Moves only with removal or upgrade of proper-lockfile.
records.notes: Integrity sha512-9LkiTwjUh6rT555DtE9rTX+BKByPfrMzEAtnlEtdEwr3Nkffwiihqe2bWADg+OQRjt9gl6ICdmB/ZFDCGAtSow==. Runtime transitive only; Pige owns the fixed lease stale/update policy.

records.name: signal-exit
records.usage: Transitive process-exit cleanup hook used by the pinned proper-lockfile runtime.
records.replacementPath: Moves only with removal or upgrade of proper-lockfile.
records.notes: Integrity sha512-wnD2ZE+l+SPC/uoS0vXeE9L1+0wuaMqKlfz9AMUo38JsyLSBWSFcHR1Rri62LZc12vLr1gb3jl7iwQhgwpAbGQ==. Runtime transitive only; lease release still verifies ownership before removal.

records.name: @types/proper-lockfile
records.usage: Build-time TypeScript declarations for the proper-lockfile adapter.
records.replacementPath: Remove if proper-lockfile ships sufficient declarations or the adapter is replaced.
records.notes: Integrity sha512-uo2ABllncSqg9F1D4nugVl9v93RmjxF6LJzQLMLDdPaXCUIDPeOJ21Gbqi43xNKzBi/WQ0Q0dICqufzQbMjipQ==. Build/test only and absent from the packaged runtime graph.

records.name: SQLite FTS5
records.usage: Lexical search over rebuildable page indexes before local embedding models are installed.
records.replacementPath: Keep Markdown scan fallback and later vector indexes available if FTS5 is unavailable on a platform.
records.notes: Pige adds CJK 2/3-gram augmentation during indexing because default FTS tokenization is not enough for CJK lexical recall.

records.name: unified
records.usage: Core processor for the Pige Markdown rendering pipeline.
records.replacementPath: Replace behind packages/markdown if another structured Markdown pipeline is adopted.
records.notes: Runs locally over vault Markdown; no user content is executed.

records.name: remark-parse
records.usage: Parse Markdown into mdast for rendering and future validation.
records.replacementPath: Replace behind packages/markdown if another structured Markdown parser is adopted.
records.notes: Raw HTML remains disabled/sanitized downstream.

records.name: remark-gfm
records.usage: Support GitHub-Flavored Markdown tables, task lists, strikethrough, and related reader behavior.
records.replacementPath: Disable GFM support or replace behind packages/markdown if needed.
records.notes: Reader-only parser extension.

records.name: remark-frontmatter
records.usage: Recognize YAML frontmatter as metadata rather than body Markdown during rendering.
records.replacementPath: Keep Pige frontmatter parsing in packages/markdown if this parser is removed.
records.notes: Frontmatter is hidden in the reader and represented through metadata.

records.name: remark-rehype
records.usage: Transform parsed Markdown AST into HTML AST for sanitized rendering.
records.replacementPath: Replace behind packages/markdown if another renderer backend is adopted.
records.notes: Do not enable dangerous raw HTML passthrough by default.

records.name: rehype-sanitize
records.usage: Sanitize rendered HTML before it crosses into the renderer UI.
records.replacementPath: Replace with an equivalent allowlist sanitizer before changing renderer behavior.
records.notes: Required because vault Markdown and captured sources are untrusted UI input.

records.name: rehype-stringify
records.usage: Serialize sanitized HTML AST for Note Reader display.
records.replacementPath: Replace behind packages/markdown if another renderer backend is adopted.
records.notes: Serializes sanitized HTML only.

records.name: actions/checkout
records.usage: Checkout repository in CI.
records.replacementPath: Use GitHub-hosted checkout equivalent if needed.

records.name: actions/upload-artifact
records.usage: Upload reviewed body-free CI reports and unsigned packageability artifacts from clean builds.
records.replacementPath: Replace with an equivalent CI artifact uploader or retain reports in the CI provider.
records.notes: Pinned to the verified v7.0.1 release commit; uploads include reviewed body-free reports plus unsigned macOS/Windows packageability artifacts from clean builds, never user vault data or credentials.

records.name: @earendil-works/pi-agent-core
records.usage: Official Pi Agent loop, event, queue, and tool lifecycle behind the sole Pige adapter.
records.replacementPath: Adopt an official compat-free Agent entry when reviewed; both Pi pins move or roll back together.
records.notes: Integrity sha512-Lvn89ko42h5ETUb6Z0Ku6ldskEqXaTdQBYvSa0+7bdG9V6rUEpXptv5e0OVZ1HDcvi8s6/2lGCQWsxKX+DFHNw==. Its unavoidable pi-ai/compat initialization is contained in the sole adapter; Pige does not use the global catalog or default dispatcher. MIT text is bundled at resources/licenses/pi-MIT.txt and attributed in NOTICE.

records.name: @earendil-works/pi-ai
records.usage: Official Pi provider/model factories and streaming for the embedded Pi Agent.
records.replacementPath: Keep the official Pi provider runtime and update in lockstep with pi-agent-core; do not add a parallel AI SDK.
records.notes: Integrity sha512-7xfLk8sANBp+bpPEbjoOZTbPxsa+++b1JXAoSJsNa3vbs9AHHEclmvg54XLQcxH+fuwaeti/g2jeIfJ+mVYLpA==. Each run creates an isolated model collection with scoped credentials and no ambient auth. Transitive @opentelemetry/api@1.9.0 is Apache-2.0 with integrity sha512-3giAOQvZiH5F9bMlMiv8+GSPMeqg0dbaeo58/0SlA9sxSqZhnUtxzX9/2FzyhS9sWQf5S0GJE0AKBrFqjpeYcg== and remains inert: Pige imports/configures no telemetry API, provider, exporter, or observability module. Every Pi update re-audits the transitive graph, side effects, network, licenses, and no-upload boundary. MIT text is bundled at resources/licenses/pi-MIT.txt and attributed in NOTICE.

records.name: OpenAI Models API
records.usage: Connection testing and model-list discovery for OpenAI-format BYOK providers.
records.replacementPath: Use the selected provider SDK once Pige pins a concrete provider package, or keep a compatible HTTP adapter.
records.notes: No source content is sent during provider connection tests.

records.name: OpenAI Responses API
records.usage: Embedded Pi Agent model turns for built-in OpenAI BYOK profiles.
records.replacementPath: Use the reviewed official Pi AI protocol adapter selected for built-in OpenAI profiles.
records.notes: Invoked only through the embedded Pi adapter after typed egress and profile binding; raw prompts and provider responses are not persisted by default.

records.name: Anthropic Models API
records.usage: Connection testing and model-list discovery for Anthropic-format BYOK providers.
records.replacementPath: Use the selected provider SDK once Pige pins a concrete provider package, or keep a compatible HTTP adapter.
records.notes: No source content is sent during provider connection tests; Anthropic-format requests include the required API-version header.

records.name: OpenAI Chat Completions API
records.usage: Embedded Pi Agent model turns for OpenAI-compatible/custom BYOK profiles.
records.replacementPath: Keep behind the reviewed Pi AI protocol adapter; replace only when upstream protocol support changes.
records.notes: Invoked only through the embedded Pi adapter for compatible/custom profiles; raw prompts and raw provider responses are not persisted by default.

records.name: Anthropic Messages API
records.usage: Embedded Pi Agent model turns for Anthropic and Anthropic-compatible BYOK profiles.
records.replacementPath: Keep behind the reviewed Pi AI protocol adapter; replace only when upstream protocol support changes.
records.notes: Invoked only through the embedded Pi adapter with the required API-version header; raw prompts and raw provider responses are not persisted by default.

records.name: yazl
records.usage: Streaming ZIP creation for .pige-backup.zip files.
records.replacementPath: Replace through the Backup Service archive adapter if cancellation, streaming, or platform packaging requirements change.
records.notes: Runs only in the main process; renderer never creates backup ZIP files.

records.name: yauzl
records.usage: ZIP restore preview/extraction and bounded OpenXML package inspection for PPTX/DOCX parser preflight.
records.replacementPath: Replace through the shared bounded archive adapter if malformed ZIP handling, streaming, or OpenXML requirements change.
records.notes: Restore validates ZIP entry paths, manifest entries, sizes, and checksums before apply. Office parser preflight validates entry names, counts, compressed/uncompressed sizes, ratios, and required package parts without extracting files to disk.

records.name: @types/yazl
records.usage: Type declarations for Backup Service ZIP creation.
records.replacementPath: Remove if yazl ships sufficient first-party TypeScript types.

records.name: @types/yauzl
records.usage: Type declarations for Backup Service ZIP preview and extraction.
records.replacementPath: Remove if yauzl ships sufficient first-party TypeScript types.

records.name: actions/setup-node
records.usage: Install Node/npm in CI.
records.replacementPath: Use runner-provided Node if pinned by image policy.

records.name: GitHub Dependabot
records.usage: Dependency security alerts and version-update pull requests.
records.replacementPath: Replace with equivalent dependency alerting and update automation if GitHub hosting changes.

records.name: github/codeql-action
records.usage: JavaScript/TypeScript code scanning.
records.replacementPath: Replace with equivalent static analysis if GitHub Actions changes.

records.name: Mammoth.js
records.usage: Local semantic DOCX conversion for headings, paragraphs, lists, tables, links, and image references.
records.replacementPath: Keep the DOCX adapter independent so a reviewed OpenXML-only extractor can replace Mammoth if semantic or security fixtures regress.
records.notes: Pure JavaScript with no native runtime. Pige disables embedded style maps and external file access, replaces inline image data with local placeholders, and never renders Mammoth HTML directly. Mammoth carries JSZip transitively for its internal DOCX reader; Pige does not import JSZip directly.

records.name: fast-xml-parser
records.usage: Parse bounded local OpenXML parts for PPTX slide order, text, notes, relationships, metadata, and media references, plus normalized Mammoth output.
records.replacementPath: Replace behind the OpenXML parser adapter if malformed XML, entity handling, or ordered-node fixtures regress.
records.notes: Pure JavaScript, ESM/CJS compatible. Parser options preserve order where needed, disable value coercion and entity processing for untrusted Office XML, and operate only on XML entries already bounded by yauzl preflight.

records.name: NAPI-RS Canvas
records.usage: Provide PDF.js 6 Node runtime primitives plus bounded native Canvas and PNG encoding for the explicit local PDF page OCR materializer.
records.replacementPath: Replace behind the PDF parser/page-renderer ports if PDF.js no longer needs these primitives or another reviewed pixel materializer is adopted.
records.notes: Pinned explicitly instead of relying on PDF.js optional dependency resolution. The separate bounded page-renderer worker uses Canvas only for selected OCR pages; platform native packages require macOS arm64/x64 and Windows x64 packaged-worker smoke.

records.name: Mozilla PDF.js generic distribution
records.usage: Local embedded-text extraction, PDF metadata/page locators, and bounded rasterization of verified OCR candidate pages through separate worker adapters.
records.replacementPath: Keep the parser adapter independent so a reviewed PDFium or Poppler worker can replace PDF.js if extraction fixtures regress.
records.notes: Pinned official Mozilla package. Node requirement is >=22.13 or >=24; Pige pins Node 24. Text extraction and page rasterization remain separate adapters with independent limits; the OCR path renders only verified candidate pages.

records.name: Mozilla Readability
records.usage: Extract article text and normalized article metadata from inert fetched HTML in the bounded web-extractor worker.
records.replacementPath: Keep the web extractor adapter typed so a reviewed article-extraction engine can replace Readability without changing Source Records.
records.notes: Readability output HTML is never rendered or persisted as trusted UI. Pige stores bounded plain text and selected metadata only.

records.name: jsdom
records.usage: Provide the inert DOM implementation used by Mozilla Readability in the bounded web-extractor worker.
records.replacementPath: Replace behind WebContentExtractorPort if security, memory, or packaging evidence no longer meets release budgets.
records.notes: Scripts and external resources are not enabled. DOM parsing runs off the main thread with input, element, output, time, and heap bounds.

records.name: Undici
records.usage: Perform main-process HTTP(S) URL capture with a per-request dispatcher that pins already validated DNS addresses.
records.replacementPath: Keep transport behind SourceFetchImplementation so a platform-reviewed HTTP client can replace it without changing capture contracts.
records.notes: The direct desktop dependency resolves its nested 8.7.0 runtime under Electron 43.1.0 / Node 24.18.0; transitive tooling may retain Undici 7. Redirect targets are manually revalidated, each hop receives a fresh address-pinned Agent, HTTP/2 stays disabled, and ambient proxy variables are not adopted.

records.name: @types/jsdom
records.usage: Type declarations for the jsdom-backed web extractor implementation.
records.replacementPath: Remove only if jsdom ships sufficient declarations for the APIs used by Pige.
records.notes: Build-only declaration package; it is not required by the packaged application runtime.

records.name: Apple Vision framework
records.usage: Run local direct-image OCR with RecognizeDocumentsRequest revision 1 and RecognizeTextRequest revision 3 fallback on macOS 26+.
records.version: macOS 26 SDK; document revision1; text revision3
records.replacementPath: Keep OCR behind OcrPort so Windows AI OCR or managed PaddleOCR can replace or supplement the platform engine.
records.notes: App-owned helper uses no cloud OCR and records exact request revisions in resources/parser-manifests/macos-vision-ocr.helper.manifest.json.

records.name: Apple ImageIO, CoreGraphics, and UniformTypeIdentifiers
records.usage: Validate image type, frame count, dimensions, and bounded thumbnail decode before Apple Vision OCR.
records.replacementPath: Keep image preflight inside the native OCR adapter so a reviewed decoder can replace platform decoding if fixtures regress.
records.notes: Runtime-provided frameworks are invoked only in the app-owned native helper with explicit file, pixel, frame, decode, and output bounds.

records.name: Apple Swift compiler and macOS 26 SDK
records.usage: Compile the app-owned macOS Vision OCR helper for each release architecture.
records.replacementPath: Keep the helper protocol and OcrPort stable if a future Objective-C or C++ build path replaces Swift.
records.notes: The generated adjacent helper manifest records the exact compiler string, target, source SHA-256, binary SHA-256, and byte size; release CI must replace local-only evidence with pinned runner/Xcode evidence.
